mnemonics: SAD Traffic: Security Associations can be combined in two ways: Adjacent (Transport) or a Distance apart (Tunnel).
keyPoints: Security Associations (SAs) can be combined into bundles to handle complex traffic flows.
The two primary methods of combination are Transport Adjacency and Iterated Tunneling.
These combinations allow for the implementation of multiple security protocols (e.g., AH and ESP) on the same packet.
commonMistakes: Assuming Transport and Tunnel modes are mutually exclusive; they can be iterated or used adjacently.
Confusing the order of operations in a bundle, which is critical for proper processing and security.
